channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: FIFA overturns red card suspension for Folarin Balogun amid political controversy
item.description: FIFA's decision to suspend the red card for Folarin Balogun following a controversial incident during the 2026 World Cup has ignited widespread debate. The incident occurred after a high-speed collisi...

item.title: Jackie Matubia's Nanny Retracts Claim, Says She Left Voluntarily
item.description: Brigid Nanjala, former nanny of Kenyan actress Jackie Matubia, posted a TikTok video contradicting her earlier claims that she was chased out of the home. In the clip, she appears emotional and sugges...

item.title: BMW Introduces New iX2 eDrive20 Electric SUV in South Africa
item.description: BMW South Africa has expanded its electric vehicle (EV) offerings by introducing the iX2 eDrive20, an entry-level compact SUV in its premium lineup. The model joins existing EV options like the iX1 xD...

item.title: KP Government Expands Legislative Privileges with Lifetime Official Passports Law
item.description: The Khyber Pakhtunkhwa government has enacted the KP Provincial Assembly (Powers, Immunities and Privileges) Act, 2026, granting lawmakers lifetime official passports and expanding their privileges. K...

item.title: New Zealand's Fossil Record Offers Clues to Marine Extinction Risks
item.description: Scientists are increasingly concerned about the potential for a sixth mass extinction, driven by human activities. Unlike past extinctions caused by natural disasters, today's crisis is linked to habi...

item.title: Manhattan high-rise evacuation due to structural concerns
item.description: Fire officials in New York City announced evacuations around a Midtown Manhattan high-rise building undergoing conversion from office space to luxury apartments after reports of falling bricks and buc...

item.title: Schengen Visa System Highlights Unequal Trust in Migration Policies
item.description: The article explores how the Schengen Area's visa system treats applicants from different African countries unequally, with Senegalese nationals facing higher rejection rates compared to Ivorians. Thr...

item.title: PAGASA forecasts Super Typhoon Inday's path and impacts on Philippines
item.description: The Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical, and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA) has given the super typhoon Bavi the local name Inday even before its entry into the Philippine Area of Respo...

item.title: Emerald HoldCo bids N6.9 billion for Beta Glass minority shares
item.description: Emerald HoldCo, a Nigerian multinational conglomerate, has launched a bid to acquire 55.2% of Beta Glass, a leading manufacturing company, through a strategic takeover offer valued at N6.9 billion. Th...

item.title: Sebastian Kris eyes contract extension as Canberra Raiders prepare for Bulldogs clash
item.description: Sebastian Kris, a key player for the Canberra Raiders, has expressed his desire to extend his contract with the club ahead of their upcoming match against the Bulldogs. The article details the Raiders...

item.title: Yas and ZTE Expand 5G Access in Tanzania to Boost Digital Inclusion
item.description: Yas Tanzania and ZTE have partnered to provide high-speed 5G internet services across Tanzania through an installment payment plan. The initiative allows customers to own a ZTE 5G router by making an ...

item.title: Belgium Players Celebrate Victory Over USA in World Cup 2026 Amid Political Controversy
item.description: Belgium's football team celebrated a 4-1 victory over the United States in the 2026 World Cup Round of 16 with a locker room dance mimicking Donald Trump's signature stiff-armed shuffle, sparking glob...

item.title: Qatar Blames Iran for Attacks on Ships in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Three tankers, including a Qatari LNG vessel, were attacked in the Strait of Hormuz, with Qatar condemning Iran for the incident. The attacks, attributed to an 'unknown projectile' and drones, have re...

item.title: New UK Study Suggests Blood Test Could Revolutionize Endometriosis Diagnosis
item.description: A groundbreaking study from the University of Edinburgh suggests that a blood test analyzing hormone levels could significantly improve the diagnosis of endometriosis. Researchers examined hormone pro...

item.title: Argentina Trails Egypt 1-0 at World Cup Round of 16
item.description: In a dramatic World Cup clash, defending champions Argentina faced an unexpected challenge from Egypt in the round of 16. The match, held at Mercedes-Benz Stadium, saw Egypt take an early lead through...

item.title: Maryland attorney general declines to charge officers in fatal shooting of autistic man
item.description: Four months after police in Maryland fatally shot Alex LaMorie, an autistic man who had contacted authorities seeking help with an extortion scam and expressed suicidal thoughts, state officials have ...

item.title: No Winners in Major Lotto Jackpot Draws on July 7, Ultra Lotto Prize Now P128M
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) reported no winners for the major jackpot prizes drawn on July 7, 2026. The Ultra Lotto 6/58 prize has increased to P128 million after no bettor matche...

item.title: Former DOJ Employees Oppose Todd Blanche's Attorney General Nomination Over Leadership Concerns
item.description: More than 1,200 former Justice Department employees have urged Senators to reject President Donald Trump's nomination of Todd Blanche as Attorney General, citing his alleged creation of a 'culture of ...

item.title: Limited Access to Safer Sex Options in South Africa: Female and Finger/Tongue Condoms in Short Supply
item.description: Community health organisations and activists in South Africa are raising concerns about the limited availability of female condoms and finger/tongue condoms, which are essential for safer sex practice...

item.title: Dangote Refinery becomes Europe's largest aviation fuel supplier, exports ₦757bn worth of jet fuel to continent
item.description: The Dangote Refinery has achieved a major milestone by becoming Europe's biggest supplier of aviation fuel for June 2026, exporting 466,000 metric tonnes (worth ₦757 billion) surpassing the United Sta...
